source: NYU Office of Global Services — Register Part-Time: Reduced Course Load (line 9-13)
body:
**F-1 and J-1 international students MUST register full-time every semester unless OGS approves a Reduced Course Load (RCL).**

Verbatim from the NYU Office of Global Services *Register Part-Time: Reduced Course Load* page:

*"All F-1 and J-1 international students must register full-time every semester or receive permission from OGS for a reduced course load (RCL), which is anything less than full-time registration."*

*"For undergraduate students a minimum of 12 credits is considered full-time enrollment; for graduate students, typically a minimum of 9 credits is considered full-time enrollment. However, you should confirm with your academic department as to what full-time enrollment is for your academic program."*

**Critical consequence — verbatim:**

*"If you register or drop below full-time without OGS permission AND the support of your academic department, you will lose the benefits of F-1 and J-1 status and you will be unable to stay and study in the US, and this may impact your eligibility for F-1 CPT or OPT or J-1 Academic Training."*

**Three RCL paths recognized by OGS:**

1. **Final semester** — when you have fewer than full-time credits remaining to complete your degree, you may apply for an RCL covering your last term.
2. **Medical RCL** — for a documented medical condition requiring leave of absence or a lighter load.
3. **Other academic reasons** — initial English-language difficulty, unfamiliarity with US teaching methods, improper course level placement. Speak to an OGS advisor during their published advising hours.

**Before any withdrawal that drops you below 12 credits:**
- Contact your academic department FIRST to confirm you remain eligible to continue your program.
- THEN contact OGS to begin the RCL request — do NOT drop first and apply after.
- OGS cannot authorize a zero-credit RCL without department confirmation that you can continue post-leave.

This is the highest-stakes academic decision you can make as an F-1/J-1 student. Always confirm with your DSO at OGS before acting.
